description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a weathered, dark greenish-brown finish. The axe head is made of metal, showing signs of wear and oxidation, and is attached securely to the handle with a traditional wedged mounting. The blade is broad and single-edged, with a flat poll on the opposite side, and the cutting edge is slightly curved for efficient chopping. The head appears to be forged, with visible rivets or fasteners reinforcing the connection to the handle. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, giving the axe a utilitarian, well-used appearance suitable for urban maintenance or emergency use.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, single-edged metal blade, showing signs of use and age.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the full profile of the metal blade.
description_view_1: A top-down view showing the narrow edge of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, again showing the curved handle and the blade's profile, with visible wear on the metal.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view focusing on the handle's length and the attachment point of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, oval cross-section, no visible grip texture, dark weathered finish.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad metal blade with a slightly curved cutting edge and flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible fasteners.
